description: Fix: plugin update check not running on config reloads — updates published to npm after server startup are never detected until server restart. Also fix startup sequencing where update data is not included in the initial sync.full to the extension.

userStories.title: Run checkForUpdates during performConfigReload and send plugins.changed with results
userStories.description:
Add checkForUpdates to performConfigReload so that config reloads (POST /reload, config file watcher, plugin install/uninstall) trigger npm update checks. After the check, if outdated plugins were found and the extension is connected, send a plugins.changed notification so the extension immediately reflects update availability.

Also fix the same sequencing issue in performReload: after checkForUpdates runs (line 480), if outdated plugins exist and extension is connected, send a plugins.changed notification.
acceptanceCriteria: performConfigReload calls checkForUpdates(state) after reloadCore, wrapped in try/catch (best-effort, same pattern as performReload line 478-483)

After checkForUpdates in performConfigReload, if state.outdatedPlugins.length > 0 and isExtensionConnected(state), send a plugins.changed notification via sendToExtension with buildConfigStatePayload(state)

After checkForUpdates in performReload (line 480), if state.outdatedPlugins.length > 0 and isExtensionConnected(state), send a plugins.changed notification via sendToExtension with buildConfigStatePayload(state)
The plugins.changed notification follows the existing pattern: { jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'plugins.changed', params: { ...buildConfigStatePayload(state) } }
Import sendToExtension and buildConfigStatePayload from extension-handlers.js — isExtensionConnected is already imported from state.js
No changes to reloadCore itself — the update check is added to the callers (performReload and performConfigReload), not the shared core

userStories.title: Add unit tests verifying performConfigReload runs checkForUpdates
userStories.description: Add unit tests in reload.test.ts that verify: (1) performConfigReload calls checkForUpdates, and (2) performConfigReload succeeds even if checkForUpdates throws.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test in reload.test.ts verifies that performConfigReload calls checkForUpdates
A new test verifies that performConfigReload still returns a valid result even if checkForUpdates throws
The tests use vitest mocking to stub checkForUpdates from version-check.js
All existing tests in reload.test.ts continue to pass
userStories.notes:
File to modify: platform/mcp-server/src/reload.test.ts

Read the existing test file first to understand the setup pattern. The file already creates temp config dirs, writes config files, and creates mock plugin dirs.

The key challenge: the existing test file imports `performConfigReload` and `performReload` directly. To mock `checkForUpdates`, you need `vi.mock('./version-check.js')` at the top of the test file.

However, be careful: `performReload` already calls `checkForUpdates` (line 480 in reload.ts). The existing tests for `performReload` may already pass without the mock because `checkForUpdates` with no npm plugins is a no-op. Adding a mock won't break them, but verify all tests still pass.

Approach:
1. Add `vi.mock('./version-check.js')` at the module level
2. Import the mocked function
3. Add a new describe block with two tests:
   - 'performConfigReload calls checkForUpdates' — verify the mock was called with state
   - 'performConfigReload succeeds when checkForUpdates throws' — mock rejects, verify result is still valid
4. Reset the mock between tests with `mockClear()`

IMPORTANT: The mock must be hoisted (vi.mock is hoisted by vitest). Do NOT use dynamic mocking inside tests — declare it at the module level.

userStories.title: Add E2E test verifying plugins.changed includes update data after reload
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that verifies the plugins.changed notification sent after a reload includes update data. This tests the new behavior where performReload sends a follow-up plugins.changed after checkForUpdates populates outdated data.
acceptanceCriteria: A new E2E test in side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts verifies that after injecting outdated data and triggering a POST /reload, the update dot reappears in the side panel
The test uses the existing test infrastructure and follows the same patterns
All existing E2E tests continue to pass
